mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict returns (p > 0.05 in regression), or 60d returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary policy regime (FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 2%) leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to higher discount rates and reduced risk appetite.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict returns (p > 0.05 in interaction term), or 20d returns in tightening regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals robust employment conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased consumer confidence and spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 105) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or INDPRO fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in GDP (GDPC1 > 2%) and retail sales (RSAFS > 450k) signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through elevated corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict returns (p > 0.05 in regression), or 60d returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A supply shock (INDPRO < 105 and CPIAUCSL > 2%) leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to stagflationary pressures and reduced corporate margins.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict returns (p > 0.05 in interaction term), or 20d returns in stagflationary regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1).
